title: Reading Practice: A Japanese Introduction
description: Read a simple Japanese self-introduction and build comprehension skills.

blocks.passage:
📖 はじめまして！(Hajimemashite!) — Nice to meet you!

わたしは さくら です。(Watashi wa Sakura desu.) — My name is Sakura.
にほんから きました。(Nihon kara kimashita.) — I am from Japan.
とうきょうに すんでいます。(Toukyou ni sundeimasu.) — I live in Tokyo.

わたしは がくせい です。(Watashi wa gakusei desu.) — I am a student.
えいごを べんきょうして います。(Eigo wo benkyou shite imasu.) — I study English.

よろしくおねがいします！(Yoroshiku onegai shimasu!) — Please treat me kindly!

さくらの すきなもの: (Sakura no sukina mono:) — Sakura likes:
すし (sushi) — Sushi
アニメ (anime) — Anime
おちゃ (ocha) — Tea

にほんは とても きれいな くに です。(Nihon wa totemo kirei na kuni desu.) — Japan is a very beautiful country.
いっしょに にほんごを べんきょうしましょう！(Issho ni Nihongo wo benkyou shimashou!) — Let's study Japanese together!

questions.q: What is Sakura's name in Japanese script?
What city does Sakura live in?
What does 'よろしくおねがいします' mean?
questions.a: Please treat me kindly / Nice to meet you

items.explanation: Yes! にほんからきました (Nihon kara kimashita) — I am from Japan.
No, Sakura lives in Tokyo. とうきょうにすんでいます (Toukyou ni sundeimasu).
Yes! Sushi (すし) is mentioned in her favorites.
No, Sakura studies English: えいごをべんきょうしています (Eigo wo benkyou shite imasu).

blocks.title: Japanese Self-Introduction — 自己紹介 (Jikoshoukai)
blocks.content: In Japan, a proper self-introduction (自己紹介 — Jikoshoukai) follows a set pattern: say はじめまして (Hajimemashite), state your name with ___です (desu), and conclude with よろしくおねがいします (Yoroshiku onegai shimasu) while bowing. This phrase literally means 'Please regard me favorably' — it doesn't have a direct English equivalent but conveys humility and a desire to build a good relationship.
